summary:
Dentures can become temporarily unstable due to initial fitting challenges as patients adjust; this often occurs within the first few hours post-insertion, explaining the symptom reported here. Thus, \boxed{D}. Agent1 concludes that the primary factor contributing to the patient’s complaint of loosely fitted dentures soon after placement is unretention caused by transitional period adaptations needed from both dental work and user acclimation. Therefore, selecting answer choice \( \boxed{\text{D}} \) accurately reflects understanding these dynamics during early use phases when stability may fluctuate before settling into more consistent performance over time. 

In summary,
\[ \boxed{\text{D}} \] addresses the immediate yet common challenge faced upon introducing newly adjusted dentures where short-term retentiomality wanes until full integration between oral tissues and appliance takes place naturally through usage experience.
